compiled_state.title: [merge-to-main.sh]: sync step pre-commit hooks re-stage files causing uncommitted-changes loop
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: During merge-to-main.sh sync phase, pre-commit hooks modify files during the merge commit, leaving them re-staged. On resume, the script sees uncommitted changes and refuses to proceed — creating an unrecoverable loop. Workaround: manual merge on the main repo. Impact: blocked session merge for ~10 minutes.

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned — sync phase's post-commit state leaves re-staged files uncleared before marking sync complete; gap left by prior fix 96c1-a4a6/794ecad2 (high confidence)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation complete. ROOT_CAUSE: After sync phase completes git merge origin/main commit on worktree, pre-commit hooks that restage files (e.g., ruff auto-formatting) leave files in worktree index; sync phase never calls 'git reset HEAD' on worktree before _state_mark_complete('sync'), so on resume the top-level dirty-check sees DIRTY_CACHED non-empty and exits 1 — unrecoverable loop. Confidence: high. Fix: add 'git reset HEAD --quiet || true' in _phase_sync after git merge call on worktree, before _state_mark_complete. Testing_mode: RED.
comments.author: Test
